key_insight: The paper shows how to compute stress accurately in localized-orbital DFT, including linear-scaling formulations, and finds that stress converges well with modest density-matrix cutoffs.
engineering_relevance: This enables reliable pressure- and volume-dependent simulations, including stable large-scale isothermal-isobaric molecular dynamics, in linear-scaling electronic structure workflows.
method: Derive stress expressions for localized orbital DFT in both exact-diagonalization and linear-scaling regimes
Implement and test the approach in the large-scale DFT code Conquest
Compare convergence of stress against energy and forces across materials with different band gaps
evidence_signal: The abstract reports convergence tests showing excellent stress convergence for modest cutoffs and stable, accurate large-scale NPT molecular dynamics.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on choosing a sufficiently large density-matrix range cutoff, which may need material-specific testing to ensure stress convergence.
paper_title: Stress calculation in linear scaling DFT: convergence and dynamics
